 {"id":"bd-2ao","title":"Implement Kitty keyboard protocol support (optional)","description":"# Implement Kitty Keyboard Protocol Support (Optional)

## Background
The plan mentions Kitty keyboard protocol in TerminalCapabilities detection and ADR-004. This is an optional enhancement that provides richer key event information.

## What Kitty Keyboard Protocol Provides
- Distinguish key press vs repeat vs release
- Modifier key events (Shift pressed, Shift released)
- Proper handling of key combinations
- Unicode codepoint of key
- Alternate key representations

## Why It's Optional (P3)
- Only supported by Kitty, WezTerm, Ghostty, foot
- Traditional CSI sequences cover most use cases
- Additional complexity for marginal benefit
- Feature-gated to avoid bloating core

## Technical Design

### Detection
```rust
impl TerminalCapabilities {
    pub fn detect() -> Self {
        // ...
        let kitty_keyboard = term.contains("kitty") ||
            std::env::var("KITTY_WINDOW_ID").is_ok() ||
            // Could also detect WezTerm, Ghostty
            false;
    }
}
```

### Protocol Activation
```rust
impl TerminalSession {
    pub fn enable_kitty_keyboard(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        // CSI > flags u
        // flags: 0b1111 = disambiguate + report event types + report alternate keys + report all keys
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[>15u")?;
        self.kitty_keyboard_active = true;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    pub fn disable_kitty_keyboard(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        // CSI < u - pop keyboard mode
        self.writer.write_all(b"\x1b[<u")?;
        self.kitty_keyboard_active = false;
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Enhanced Event Type
```rust
pub struct KeyEvent {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
    pub kind: KeyEventKind,  // Press, Repeat, Release
    // Kitty-only extensions:
    pub base_codepoint: Option<char>,
    pub shifted_key: Option<char>,
}

pub enum KeyEventKind {
    Press,   // Key pressed
    Repeat,  // Key held down (auto-repeat)
    Release, // Key released
}
```

### Parser Extension
```rust
impl InputParser {
    fn parse_kitty_key(&mut self, seq: &[u8]) -> Option<Event> {
        // CSI unicode-codepoint ; modifiers ; event-type u
        // Parse the enhanced sequence
        // ...
    }
}
```

## Graceful Degradation
- If Kitty protocol not available, fall back to standard CSI
- KeyEventKind defaults to Press
- Optional fields are None for legacy input

## Tests Required
- [ ] Detection works for Kitty terminal
- [ ] Protocol activation/deactivation
- [ ] Parse enhanced key sequences
- [ ] Modifier key press/release events
- [ ] Graceful fallback when not supported
- [ ] RAII cleanup on exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Feature-gated behind `kitty-keyboard` feature
- [ ] Detection in TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] Activation/deactivation in TerminalSession
- [ ] Enhanced KeyEvent fields
- [ ] Parser handles Kitty sequences
- [ ] Falls back gracefully
- [ ] Documented in compatibility matrix

-{"id":"bd-2axgy","title":"task(pane-terminal): define and implement terminal mouse-capture policy for pane interactions","description":"Implement terminal policy controlling when mouse capture is enabled for pane interactions, with explicit behavior for inline vs alt-screen modes and user overrides.\n\nDeliverables\n- Config surface for capture modes (auto/on/off).\n- Inline-safe defaults preserving scrollback unless explicitly opted in.\n- Runtime policy docs and in-app status indicators.\n\nAcceptance\n- Policy is predictable, testable, and safe by default.\n- Capture toggles do not leak terminal state on crash/exit.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Implement scoped terminal adapter behavior while preserving terminal lifecycle safety.\n2) Add unit/integration tests for event mapping, geometry routing, and capability variation.\n3) Add or extend PTY E2E coverage for relevant workflows.\n4) Ensure logs capture raw event, semantic event, geometry decision, and cleanup state.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":150,"created_at":"2026-02-11T05:32:40.948343071Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T18:28:50.253458217Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["inline","input-policy","pane","terminal"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2axgy","depends_on_id":"bd-23pre","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:32:48.426289400Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2axgy","depends_on_id":"bd-2tp51","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:32:46.257608156Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2axgy","depends_on_id":"bd-x9lqw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T05:32:42.711487803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-2b82","title":"Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration","description":"## Background
The visual_effects screen needs to showcase all new text effects in an impressive, navigable demo that serves as both documentation and a selling point for FrankenTUI.

## Implementation

### Navigation Structure
```
Tab 1: Gradients    - Horizontal, Vertical, Diagonal, Radial, Animated
Tab 2: Animations   - Wave, Bounce, Shake, Cascade, Pulse, Breathing
Tab 3: Typography   - Shadow, Glow, Outline, Mirror, ASCII Art
Tab 4: Special FX   - Glitch, Chromatic, Scanline, Particle, Matrix
Tab 5: Presets      - All 10 presets with descriptions
Tab 6: Combinations - User can toggle effects on/off
```

### Key Features
1. Spacebar cycles through effects within current tab
2. TransitionOverlay shows effect name + description on switch
3. Number keys 1-6 switch tabs
4. 'e' key toggles easing comparison mode
5. 'c' key enters combination builder

### Visual Layout
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ [Gradients] [Animations] [Typography] ...    │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│                                             │
│         EFFECT DEMO TEXT                    │
│         (with current effect)               │
│                                             │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Effect: Rainbow Gradient                    │
│ Speed: 0.3  │  Easing: EaseInOut           │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ Space: next │ ←→: adjust │ Tab: category   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### ASCII Art Header
Use AsciiArtText with Block style for 'EFFECTS' title, animated gradient.

## Files
- crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/visual_effects.rs

## Testing

### Unit Tests
- test_tab_navigation: Keys switch tabs correctly
- test_effect_cycling: Spacebar advances effect index
- test_transition_triggers: Effect change starts transition

### E2E Tests (in scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh)
```bash
# Navigate through all tabs and effects
echo 'Testing visual effects screen navigation...'
# Send key sequence: 1,space,space,2,space,3...
# Verify no panics, all effects render
```

## Acceptance
- All 6 tabs implemented
- All effects showcased
- Smooth transitions between effects
- No rendering glitches
- Help text updated with new keybindings

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Demo Showcase Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode
